title: Cerasus crataegifolia
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Cerasus crataegifolia. However, I found that it may be a synonym for the plant Cerasus crataegifolia is now known as  Cerasus pallasiana.  But I was able to find information on the Prunus crataegifolia or Cerasus pallasiana, which is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family (Rosaceae). 

Cerasus pallasiana has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections.

The bioactive constituents of Cerasus pallasiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

Cerasus pallasiana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures. It is often used to make teas and infusions to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cerasus crataegifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to East Asia. The plant is placed in the genus Cerasus, which includes other cherry species. Cerasus crataegifolia is closely related to other deciduous shrubs and trees within the family Rosaceae. As a member of this family, it is also related to rose and apple species.
properties: Cerasus crataegifolia, a lesser-known species within the Prunus genus, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. Research has shown that the plant may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. The extracts of Cerasus crataegifolia have been found to possess antimicrobial properties, making it a possible candidate for the development of new antimicrobial agents. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers may have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. Further research is necessary to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Cerasus crataegifolia.
constituents: Cerasus crataegifolia, also known as the wild cherry, contains a variety of active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. It also contains glycosides, such as amygdalin, which is a cyanogenic glycoside that releases cyanide when metabolized. Furthermore, Cerasus crataegifolia contains various phenolic acids, including chlorogenic and caffeic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, the plant contains iridoid glycosides, such as crataegus sambucifolia saponins, which may contribute to its cardiovascular benefits.
